rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 24, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERCIA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift burden of proof. Plantiff's standard interrogatory responses identify several witnesses and defendant has failed to demonstrate, either by special interrogatories or depositions of those witnesses, that plaintiff is unable to prove her case. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD L MILLER VS. BERNARD N WOLF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 24, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. In the Court's prior orders, the Court has spelled out why the complaint is barred by the statute of limitations, CCP 340.6. The 4th amended complaint adds no additional facts and for the reasons stated, plaintiff's claim is barred. Plaintiff's 2nd cause of action is nothing more than a restatement of plaintiff's legal malpractice claim with a reference to B & P Code 17200. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts that support such a claim. Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal App 4th 612, 619. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHILDREN'S DAY SCHOOL VS. ROBERT J MCCARTHY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 24, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to anser. The record reflects that the demurrer was not timely filed. In addition, "In order for the bar of the statute of limitations to be raised by demurrer, the defect must clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the complaint; it is not enough that the complaint shows that the action may be barred." Marshall v. Gibson Dunnis, Crutcher (1995) 37 Cal App 4th 1397, 1403. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JEANETTE RAMOS VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 24, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS JOHN STEWART COMPANY, ANA BOLTON, HOTEL ISABEL AND HOTEL GRAND SOUTHERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred by collateral estoppel. The issue of plaintiff failing to make rent payments was the central issue in the prior case. As a consequence, plaintiff needed to present evidence at that time as to any defense she had for her failure to pay rent. =(302/PJM/xAM)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To San Joaquin County Superior Cour, Stockton Branch; Request For Judicial Notice; Supporting Declaration Of Scottbernasconi; Supporting Declaration Of Counsel And Exhibit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 24, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Transfer Venue To San Joaquin County Superior Court, Stockton Branch. The motion is granted. CCP 392 does not apply to this dispute. Under CCP 395.5, venue is proper in San Joaquin County. =(302/PJM)
